Consider how you check if two trees are completely identical (similar to the 'Same Tree' problem).
A tree is a subtree of another if either they are exactly identical, or the subtree matches the left child of the root, or the subtree matches the right child of the root.
You can solve this recursively by combining a traversal function with an `isSameTree` verification function.
Be careful with null cases. If the main tree is null, we can't find the subtree!
